headline: 35+ contracts catalogued across Ethereum, Base, and Arbitrum; core staking, restaking, governance, and treasury infrastructure identified

findings.text: Deployed Contracts page (etherfi.gitbook.io/etherfi/contracts-and-integrations/deployed-contracts) lists 29 Ethereum mainnet contracts including eETH, weETH, ETHFI, LiquidityPool, EtherFiTimelock, EtherFiAdmin, and RoleRegistry; plus cross-chain weETH/ETHFI/eBTC token deployments on Base, Arbitrum, Linea, Scroll, Blast, and 11+ other chains; plus Scroll-exclusive Cash contracts and HyperEVM beHYPE contracts.

GitHub audit folder (github.com/etherfi-protocol/smart-contracts/tree/master/audits) lists 13 public audit reports: CertiK (Feb 2023), Omniscia (May 2023), Nethermind (July 2023), Solidified (Oct 2023), Hats Finance (Dec 2023), Zellic (Jan & Mar 2024), Decurity (Apr 2024), Halborn (June & Aug 2024), and Paladin (Sept 2024).

GitHub organization etherfi-protocol hosts 6+ public repositories: smart-contracts (primary, 134 stars, updated Apr 2026), cash-v3, weETH-cross-chain, avs-smart-contracts, ethfi-wormhole, and beHYPE; README documents staking, liquid vaults, and cash products.

Protocol operates across Ethereum (mainnet), Base, and Arbitrum as specified; weETH token deployed identically on Base and BSC (0x04C0599Ae5A44757c0af6F9eC3b93da8976c150A); ETHFI governance token on Ethereum (0xFe0c30065B384F05761f15d0CC899D4F9F9Cc0eB), Arbitrum (0x7189fb5B6504bbfF6a852B13B7B82a3c118fDc27), and Base (0x6C240DDA6b5c336DF09A4D011139beAAa1eA2Aa2).

Key admin/control contracts identified: EtherFiAdmin (0x0EF8fa4760Db8f5Cd4d993f3e3416f30f942D705), EtherFiTimelock (0x9f26d4C958fD811A1F59B01B86Be7dFFc9d20761), RoleRegistry (0x62247D29B4B9BECf4BB73E0c722cf6445cfC7cE9), Treasury (0x6329004E903B7F420245E7aF3f355186f2432466), EtherFiOracle (0x57AaF0004C716388B21795431CD7D5f9D3Bb6a41), Node Operator Manager (0xd5edf7730ABAd812247F6F54D7bd31a52554e35E), Membership Manager (0x3d320286E014C3e1ce99Af6d6B00f0C1D63E3000), and AvsOperatorsManager (0x2093Bbb221f1d8C7c932c32ee28Be6dEe4a37A6a).

Governance infrastructure: ETHFI token functions as governance token (1B supply, fixed mint); Snapshot voting (snapshot.box/#/s:etherfi-dao.eth) with 1M ETHFI quorum requirement; governance.ether.fi forum for discussion; Agora (vote.ether.fi) transitioning to Snapshot; ether.fi Foundation with Proposer and Multi-Sig Committee roles.

Deployed Contracts page also lists ETHFI Staking contract on Ethereum, Arbitrum, and Base (0x86B5780b606940Eb59A062aA85a07959518c0161), claim contracts (13 tranches across seasons 1-3), and Liquid Vault contracts (referenced in separate docs).

rationale.verdict: Catalogued 35+ contract addresses across Ethereum mainnet, Base, and Arbitrum from official deployed-contracts documentation, GitHub source repositories (6+ public repos), and audit reports (13 audits by tier-1 firms). Core staking/restaking contracts (eETH, weETH, LiquidityPool), governance/admin infrastructure (ETHFI, Timelock, Admin, RoleRegistry, Oracle), treasury, and claim/reward contracts identified and role-classified. Protocol operates on 3 pinned chains with additional cross-chain deployments documented.

evidence.shows: Authoritative deployed-contracts page listing 29 Ethereum mainnet contracts with addresses and roles, plus cross-chain deployments of weETH (Base, Arbitrum, Linea, Scroll, Blast, Avalanche, and 10+ others), ETHFI (Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base), eBTC (Ethereum, Base, Arbitrum), Cash contracts on Scroll, beHYPE on HyperEVM, ETHFI Staking addresses, and claim/reward contracts.

13 audit reports by CertiK (Feb 2023), Omniscia (May 2023), Nethermind (July 2023), Solidified (Oct 2023), Hats Finance (Dec 2023), Zellic (Jan & Mar 2024), Decurity (Apr 2024), Halborn (June & Aug 2024), Paladin (Sept 2024).

etherfi-protocol GitHub organization with 6+ public repositories: smart-contracts (primary, 134 stars, updated Apr 2026), cash-v3, weETH-cross-chain, avs-smart-contracts, ethfi-wormhole, beHYPE. README documents key products: Staking, Liquid Vaults, Cash with DVT and EigenLayer integration.

Official website confirms protocol name, product line (Stake, Liquid, Cash), mentions GitHub (https://github.com/etherfi-protocol), audits (Certik, Certora, HatsFinance, Nethermind, Omniscia, Solidified), and bug bounty (https://immunefi.com/bug-bounty/etherfi/); links to docs at etherfi.gitbook.io.

Technical overview of core contracts: LiquidityPool (validator creation, deposit flow, reward accrual), eETH/weETH (rebasing and non-rebasing liquid staking tokens), EtherFiOracle (reward reporting), Membership NFT/NFT Manager, Node Operator registry and management, RoleRegistry (permission control), Timelock (upgrade governance).

ETHFI governance token details: 1B fixed supply, Snapshot voting via etherfi-dao.eth, 1M ETHFI quorum, delegation via ETHFI staking contract; users earn governance points and participate in DAO votes; multi-chain ETHFI staking on Ethereum, Arbitrum, Base.

ether.fi Foundation governance model: ETHFI token gives community mechanism to contribute and influence protocol growth; Foundation has Proposer and Multi-Sig Committee roles; constitutional documents mentioned.

unknowns: D1: Block-explorer search on Etherscan/Basescan/Arbiscan not performed for name-tag discovery; pre-built surfacer URLs not provided in context (address_book null) so no on-chain read-API queries executed.
D6: Factory discovery not performed; LiquidityPool contract may manage validator creation dynamically but enumeration not executed via read-API (no pool enumeration method called).

D8: Multisig composition and signer identities for EtherFiAdmin and Multi-Sig Committee roles not resolved; actor_class for governance/multisig addresses recorded as 'unknown' pending on-chain verification.

protocol_metadata.about: ether.fi is a decentralized, non-custodial liquid restaking protocol where users deposit ETH to mint eETH, a rebasing liquid staking token that automatically accrues staking and restaking rewards. Validators are operated by permissioned or solo node operators using Distributed Validator Technology (DVT) via SSV Network. The protocol restakes pooled ETH on EigenLayer through an oracle-driven mechanism. Governance is managed by the ETHFI token via Snapshot voting with a 1M ETHFI quorum.
